zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Gobion於大約1165年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾霍頓時，他的父親Richard de Gubion20歲，母親Beatrice de Lucelle21歲。他和Agnes de Merlay至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他於1215年被列為surety。他去世於1230年12月29日在法國米迪-列內斯享年66歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gobion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Bhola, Boodram, Mohan, Narine, Parmanand, Pooran, Shiva.  French Andre, Marcelle, Pierre.
French: from a pet form of   Gobert  .

variants.name: Gobert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Antoine, Marie-France, Monique.

French, German, and English (London): from a medieval personal name, Godebert, composed of the ancient Germanic elements gōd ‘good’ or god, got ‘god’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. The name was popularized in England by the Normans, and probably absorbed an Old English form Godbeorht.

variants.name: Goben
meanings: Altered form of Gobin , a surname of English or French origin. In some cases however, the surname Goben (possibly of German origin; see 2 below) was changed to Gobin.
German (Göben): habitational name from Gobbin on Rügen Island (Pomerania) or perhaps from any of several places called Goben in Bavaria.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Gobion was born about 1165, in Horton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard de Gubion, was 20 and his mother, Beatrice de Lucelle, was 21. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Agnes de Merlay. In 1215, at the age of 51, his occupation is listed as surety. He died on 29 December 1230, in Midi-Pyrénées, France, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Gobion est né environ 1165, à Horton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard de Gubion, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Beatrice de Lucelle, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Agnes de Merlay. En 1215, à l’âge de 51 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est surety. Il est décédé le 29 décembre 1230, à Midi-Pyrénées, France, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Gobion um 1165 in Horton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Gubion 20 und seine Mutter Beatrice de Lucelle 21. Er hatte mit Agnes de Merlay mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1215, als er 51 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als surety aufgeführt. Er starb am 29. Dezember 1230 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Midi-Pyrénées, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Gobion è nato il circa 1165 nel Horton, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Richard de Gubion, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Beatrice de Lucelle, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Agnes de Merlay. Nel 1215, all'età di 51 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come surety. Morì il 29 dicembre 1230, nel Midi-Pyrénées, Francia, all'età di 66 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Gobion(이)가 대략 1165년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 호르턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Gubion(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrice de Lucelle(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Agnes de Merlay(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1215년, 51세의 나이로 그의 직업은 surety이었다. 그는 1230년 12월 29일 프랑스 미디-p이ee스에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Gobion nasceu aproximadamente 1165, em Horton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard de Gubion, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice de Lucelle, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas com Agnes de Merlay. Em 1215, com 51 anos, sua profissão aparece como surety. Ele faleceu em 29 de dezembro de 1230, em Midi-Pyrénées, França, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Gobion родился около 1165 г. в местности хауртон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard de Gubion было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrice de Lucelle, 21. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Agnes de Merlay. В 1215 году, в возрасте 51 год,  он работал по профессии surety. Он умер 29 декабря 1230 г. в местности миди-пирeнeесь, Франция в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Gobion nació aproximadamente 1165, en Horton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard de Gubion, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Beatrice de Lucelle, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas con Agnes de Merlay. En 1215, a la edad de 51 años, su ocupación registrada era surety. Murió el 29 de diciembre de 1230, en Mediodía-Pirineos, Francia, a la edad de 66 años.
